message:
feat(cli): add am doctor fix subcommand with automatic remediation for 6 fixable checks

New feature and bug fixes:

1. am doctor fix subcommand (~610 lines in cli/lib.rs):
   - Automatic remediation for 6 fixable checks: legacy_python_alias
     (comments out alias in shell rc files), path_order (appends
     ~/.local/bin to PATH), mcp_config (rewrites Python->Rust binary
     with .json.bak backup), storage_root_git_index_lock (removes
     stale .git/index.lock), guard_hooks (installs pre-commit hooks),
     wal_mode (enables WAL on SQLite DB)
   - Supports --dry-run preview, -y/--yes auto-confirm, --json output
   - Summary with fixed/failed/skipped counts, exit 1 on failures
   - 9 unit tests covering flag parsing, alias commenting, PATH
     append idempotency, MCP config rewrite, and FIXABLE_CHECKS
     completeness

2. Bug fixes:
   - extract_body_after_frontmatter: use trim_start_matches instead
     of trim() to preserve trailing whitespace in reconstructed
     message bodies (4 new tests for fidelity)
   - full_reindex: clamp batch_size to .max(1) preventing zero-batch
     infinite loops
   - 4 call sites: Path::is_absolute() instead of starts_with('/')
     for cross-platform path detection (cli/lib.rs, share/crypto.rs,
     tools/resources.rs)

3. Improvements:
   - Outbox ack_status shows granular "done", "partial (N/M)",
     "acked (N)" instead of bare count
   - Contacts screen deduplicates expensive graph metric computations
   - Suppress dead_code warning on fetch_reservation_snapshots
   - Fix _tmp_dir naming in truthfulness integration tests

Update beads: br-28mgh.7.5 claimed, br-28mgh.7.6 closed.
Add futures-lite dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -629,13 +629,13 @@
 {"id":"br-28mgh.6.2","title":"T6.2: Download and verify new binary","description":"Download the new release binary and verify its integrity.

Flow:
1. Determine platform target (same logic as install.sh detect_platform)
2. Construct download URL from release assets
3. Download tarball to temp directory
4. Verify SHA256 checksum (download .sha256 file)
5. Extract binaries from tarball
6. Return paths to extracted binaries

Use asupersync HttpClient for downloads (already available in the crate).
Show progress indicator if terminal is attached.

Depends on: T6.1","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:19:43.328972043Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:49:17.714757780Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:49:17.714684212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.6.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.6.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.6.3","title":"T6.3: Atomic in-place binary replacement","description":"Replace the running binary with the new version atomically.

Challenge: On Unix, a running binary can be replaced while executing
(the OS keeps the old inode alive until the process exits). On Windows,
the running binary is locked and cannot be replaced.

Unix strategy:
1. Write new binary to $DEST/.am.new.tmp
2. chmod 755
3. Rename old binary to $DEST/.am.old.tmp (backup)
4. Rename new binary to $DEST/am (atomic)
5. Same for mcp-agent-mail
6. Clean up .old.tmp files
7. Print "Update complete. Restart am to use the new version."

Windows strategy:
1. Write new binary to %DEST%/am.new.exe
2. Create update.bat script that:
   a. Waits for current process to exit (tasklist loop)
   b. Renames am.exe to am.old.exe  
   c. Renames am.new.exe to am.exe
   d. Deletes am.old.exe
   e. Deletes itself
3. Launch update.bat detached and exit

Depends on: T6.2","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:19:43.632460514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:52:35.569716810Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:52:35.569641940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.6.3","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.6.3","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":316,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.6.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: On macOS, replacing a binary may trigger Gatekeeper quarantine on the new binary. The self-update should: (1) Copy the quarantine xattr from old binary to new if present, (2) Or use xattr -d com.apple.quarantine on the new binary, (3) Test that the updated binary actually runs without macOS blocking it. Also on macOS with SIP, /usr/local/bin may be protected - verify write access before attempting.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.6.4","title":"T6.4: Self-update CLI command wiring","description":"Wire the self-update functionality into the CLI command structure.

Commands:
  am self-update             # Check and install update
  am self-update --check     # Just check, do not install
  am self-update --force     # Reinstall current version
  am self-update --version X # Install specific version

Output:
  Checking for updates... (spinner)
  Current version: 0.1.0
  Latest version: 0.1.1
  Downloading mcp-agent-mail-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.xz... (progress)
  Verifying checksum... ok
  Installing... ok
  Updated from 0.1.0 to 0.1.1. Restart am to use the new version.

Also: `am --version` should print a "update available" hint if a newer
version was detected (cache the check result for 24 hours in
~/.cache/mcp-agent-mail/update-check.json).

-{"id":"br-28mgh.7","title":"T7: Doctor Command Enhancement","description":"Track 7: Doctor Command Enhancement

The `am doctor` command should comprehensively verify the installation state and detect
any issues, including Python remnants, PATH conflicts, MCP config problems, database
format issues, and more.

## Scope
- Check that `am` resolves to the Rust binary (not an alias or Python)
- Check PATH order (DEST comes before any Python paths)
- Check for residual Python am alias in shell rc files
- Check MCP configs point to Rust binary
- Check database format (i64 vs TEXT timestamps)
- Check storage root accessibility and Git repo health
- Check for stale Python processes
- Check binary version matches expected
- Check for available updates
- Print remediation steps for each issue found
- Exit code reflects health (0=healthy, 1=issues found)

## Design
Each check is a separate function returning (status, message, fix_hint).
Doctor runs all checks and prints a summary table.
With --fix flag, doctor can attempt automatic remediation.","notes":"2026-03-02 CreamRidge progress: implemented doctor-check extensions in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs for legacy Python alias residue detection (shell rc scan), stale legacy Python process detection (ps probe), binary version verification, and cached update-awareness check; added unit tests for alias/process classifiers. rch validation: cargo fmt --check PASS; earlier workspace check/clippy passed, but subsequent targeted cargo test runs are blocked by current /dp/asupersync compile API mismatch (connect signature/cx.now).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CreamRidge","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.645195803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:59.371415393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.7","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Track complete.","created_at":"2026-03-03T05:33:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.7.1","title":"T7.1: Check am binary resolution (alias vs binary)","description":"The most critical doctor check: verify that `am` resolves to the Rust binary.

Checks:
1. Run `type am` equivalent -- is it an alias, function, or binary?
2. If binary, verify path is the expected Rust installation path
3. If alias, report the alias definition and which rc file it lives in
4. Verify `am --version` returns the expected Rust version string
5. Check that the binary at the resolved path is actually an ELF/Mach-O (not a script)

Status output:
  [PASS] am resolves to /Users/jeff/.local/bin/am (Rust v0.1.0)
  [FAIL] am is aliased to cd /Users/jeff/mcp_agent_mail && scripts/run_server_with_token.sh
         Fix: Run `unalias am` or edit ~/.zshrc to remove the Python alias
         The Rust binary is installed at ~/.local/bin/am but the alias takes precedence.

Implementation note: From within Rust, we cannot directly check shell aliases.
Use `bash -lc "type am 2>&1"` and `zsh -lc "type am 2>&1"` to check both shells.
Parse output: "am is aliased to..." vs "am is /path/to/am" vs "am is a function".

File: crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/doctor.rs (enhance existing doctor command)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:20:17.808781714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:33:56.380547886Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:33:56.380475781Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.7.1","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":317,"issue_id":"br-28mgh.7.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW: When checking alias resolution, also handle the case where am is invoked from a non-login shell (e.g., from within an IDE terminal or a subprocess). Some terminals source .bashrc but not .bash_profile. Test both: bash -lc (login) and bash -c (non-login). Also consider: the MCP server binary is invoked directly by agent tools (not through a shell), so alias shadowing only affects the CLI user.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.7.2","title":"T7.2: Check PATH order","description":"Verify that the Rust install directory appears early enough in PATH.

Checks:
1. Is ~/.local/bin (or configured dest) in PATH?
2. If yes, what position? Are there earlier paths that might shadow it?
3. Are there any Python-related paths before it that contain an am binary?
4. Check: which -a am (all matches in PATH order)

Status output:
  [PASS] PATH includes ~/.local/bin (position 3 of 12)
  [WARN] ~/.local/bin is in PATH but after /usr/local/bin which also has am
         Fix: Move ~/.local/bin earlier in PATH in your shell config
  [FAIL] ~/.local/bin is NOT in PATH
         Fix: Add to ~/.zshrc: export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

 {"id":"br-28mgh.7.3","title":"T7.3: Check MCP config health","description":"Verify MCP configurations point to the Rust binary.

For each detected agent tool:
1. Check if MCP config file exists
2. Parse JSON and find mcp-agent-mail entry
3. Verify command points to Rust binary (not Python)
4. Verify the binary at that path actually exists and is executable
5. Verify bearer token is set and matches across configs
6. Check for stale/orphaned MCP entries

Status output:
  [PASS] Claude Code: mcp-agent-mail -> /Users/jeff/.local/bin/mcp-agent-mail
  [FAIL] Codex CLI: mcp-agent-mail -> python -m mcp_agent_mail (Python!)
         Fix: Run `am setup-mcp` or manually update ~/.codex/config.json
  [SKIP] Gemini CLI: not installed
  [WARN] Claude Code: bearer token mismatch with server .env

 {"id":"br-28mgh.7.4","title":"T7.4: Check database format and health","description":"Verify the database is in the correct format and healthy.

Checks:
1. Database file exists and is readable
2. Timestamp format is i64 (not TEXT) -- uses T3.1 detection
3. Schema version is current (all migrations applied)
4. FTS5 virtual tables exist and are functional
5. Integrity check: PRAGMA integrity_check
6. WAL mode is enabled
7. Row counts per table (sanity check)
8. Last message timestamp (is the data recent or stale?)

Status output:
  [PASS] Database: ~/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo/storage.sqlite3 (2.1 MB)
  [PASS] Timestamp format: i64 microseconds (Rust native)
  [PASS] Schema version: v3 (current)
  [PASS] Integrity check: ok
  [WARN] Database has Python TEXT timestamps - run `am migrate` to convert
  [FAIL] Database not found at expected path

Builds on existing timestamp_format check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.7.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.7.4","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-28mgh.7.5","title":"T7.5: Doctor --fix automatic remediation","description":"Add --fix flag to `am doctor` that attempts automatic remediation.

For each fixable issue:
- Python alias found -> Comment it out (reuse T1.4 logic)
- PATH missing -> Append to shell rc (reuse T2.1 logic)
- MCP config points to Python -> Update to Rust (reuse T4.2 logic)
- Database needs migration -> Run migration (reuse T3.3 logic)
- Bearer token mismatch -> Sync tokens across configs

Each fix:
1. Print what will be changed
2. In interactive mode, prompt for confirmation
3. Apply the fix
4. Re-run the check to verify
5. Report success/failure

CLI:
  am doctor          # Check only, print issues
  am doctor --fix    # Attempt automatic remediation
  am doctor --json   # Machine-readable output

Exit codes:
  0: All checks pass (or all fixes succeeded)
  1: Issues found (check mode) or fixes failed
  2: Some checks could not be run (missing permissions, etc.)

 {"id":"br-28mgh.8","title":"T8: Testing and Validation","description":"Track 8: Testing and Validation

End-to-end tests that verify the entire install-migrate-run pipeline works correctly
across platforms. This includes both automated tests and a manual test checklist.

## Scope
- E2E test: fresh install on clean system (no Python)
- E2E test: install over existing Python installation
- E2E test: database migration (real Python database)
- E2E test: MCP config takeover
- E2E test: self-update from v0.1.0 to v0.1.1
- E2E test: uninstall and rollback
- E2E test: doctor command detects all issues
- Docker-based CI test matrix (ubuntu, fedora, macOS-like)
- Manual test checklist for release validation
- Integration test for shell alias detection across bash/zsh/fish

## Key Constraint
Tests must be idempotent and leave no side effects (use tmpdir/Docker).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.941206491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.941206491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.8","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.8.1","title":"T8.1: E2E test - fresh install on clean system","description":"E2E test that verifies fresh installation on a system with no prior mcp-agent-mail.

Test in Docker container (ubuntu:22.04):
1. Start with clean system (no Python, no Rust am)
2. Run: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash
3. Verify: am --version returns Rust version
4. Verify: mcp-agent-mail --version returns Rust version
5. Verify: am is a binary, not an alias
6. Verify: PATH includes ~/.local/bin
7. Verify: am doctor reports all green
8. Verify: am serve-stdio responds to MCP handshake

Also test macOS-like environment (homebrew paths, zsh as default shell).

For macOS-like env, consider testing with Homebrew's bash vs system bash since macOS ships with old bash 3.x.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:13:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.8.2","title":"T8.2: E2E test - install over Python","description":"E2E test that verifies installation over an existing Python mcp-agent-mail.

Test in Docker:
1. Set up Python mcp-agent-mail (clone, venv, alias in .zshrc)
2. Create test data: project, agent, messages, reservations
3. Run Rust installer: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash
4. Verify: am --version returns Rust version (NOT Python)
5. Verify: Python alias is commented out in .zshrc
6. Verify: Database migrated (timestamps are i64)
7. Verify: All test data accessible via Rust CLI
8. Verify: MCP configs updated to Rust binary
9. Verify: am doctor reports all green
10. Verify: Original database backed up

This is THE critical test -- it validates the entire migration pipeline end-to-end.
